message:
Fix Streamdown regex literals that break Turbopack parser Turbopack's ECMAScript parser rejects regex literals containing raw newlines (the /.../ syntax must be single-line). Three fixes: 1. Code block splitter (line 21): Changed from a regex literal /```(\w+)?\n([\s\S]*?)```/g to new RegExp() constructor which accepts \n as an escape sequence in the string argument. 2. List wrapper (line 106): Replaced the /s (dotAll) flag—which also requires ES2018 target and fails TypeScript compilation— with an explicit [\s\S]*? character class inside a non-dotAll regex. The pattern now matches consecutive
  • ...
    • groups and wraps them in a single
      . 3. Newline-to-break replacements (lines 109-110): Replaced raw newlines inside regex literals with proper \n escape sequences for both double-newline (paragraph break → div) and single- newline (line break →
      ) patterns. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6
